task_title: Construct realizations for every beta > sqrt(3) in the exact clean PL category
notes: The discretization and pullback are proved fully. The only imported input is the standard smooth embedded full-width ruled paper Möbius band for each μ>√3; without accepting that theorem, it is exactly the remaining existence assertion.

task_title: Prove lower bound via PL bend circle and weak T-pattern
notes: Complete proof supplied. The main possible gaps are addressed: quotient finiteness and PL embedding, fan degeneracies at boundary vertices, continuity through side-edge leaf identifications, the oriented-line lemma, displacement bound |t| < beta, and the shared-endpoint case in the metric argument.

task_title: Red-team and repair the PL lower-bound proof
notes: The lower-bound proof is watertight after making the quotient PL structure precise and explicitly handling the leaf-graph, oriented-line, displacement, boundary-arc, and metric degeneracies. This proves the lower-bound half beta > sqrt(3); no upper construction for the infimum statement was addressed here.

task_title: Remove the black box from the upper construction or make the citation/reduction exact
notes: The upper-bound half is complete unconditionally. Route B explains the exact obstruction to the minimal triangular model; subdivisions are possible in principle, and the Sadowsky/secant construction supplies a controlled subdivision with positive clearance.
The upper-bound construction is complete modulo the explicitly cited Sadowsky input and the standard C1 tubular-neighborhood PL approximation fact. No additional smooth paper-band black box is used.
The proof survives as a rigorous proof scheme. The final write-up should promote the Sadowsky construction and invariant secant approximation to explicit lemmas with the hypotheses listed above.

The upper bound is complete modulo the explicitly allowed Sadowsky construction. The secant approximation has been made invariant, clean, length-controlled, and quotient-injective in the required PL category.

task_title: Referee-tight lower-bound proof with PL degeneracies
notes: The lower-bound half is proved in referee-ready form, including PL refinement, quotient finiteness, support-line degeneracies, shared endpoint cases, and the metric inequalities using boundary arc lengths rather than a minimal horizontal displacement. The upper construction for realizing beta arbitrarily close to sqrt(3) is not addressed here.

task_title: Self-contained Sadowsky input for the upper bound
notes: The upper-bound proof can now cite a precise minimal Sadowsky input instead of a vague smooth-band black box. All parameter choices and all consequences needed for the PL secant construction have been derived explicitly from the Sadowsky geometry; only the classical 3D closure of the three-cylinder scaffold remains external.

task_title: Skeptical referee check of the complete proof
notes: No fatal gap was found conditional on the Sadowsky input. The main repairable point was making the secant PL embeddedness argument explicit; with the planar/cylindrical structure, C1 seam control, and positive clearance, this patch is valid.

task_title: Standalone invariant secant PL approximation lemma
notes: The secant PL approximation theorem needed for the Sadowsky upper bound is now formalized. The only remaining external input for the full upper bound is the existence of the Sadowsky-type embedded planar/cylindrical Möbius band satisfying these hypotheses.

task_title: Attempt direct finite PL upper construction without Sadowsky
notes: The Sadowsky black box is eliminated for the upper bound. Combining this direct construction with the already established lower-bound argument gives the infimum sqrt(3).

task_title: Red-team and repair the direct 9-periodic PL upper construction
notes: No flaw was found in the 9-periodic construction. The proof above supplies the missing rigorous index checks, quotient/lift verification, embeddedness proof, and all edge-length computations for the upper bound beta > sqrt(3). The lower bound for the infimum is separate and is not reproved here.

task_title: Referee-tight lower-bound proof β realized implies β > √3
notes: The lower-bound implication is proved with PL finiteness, quotient embeddedness, leaf-graph connectivity, oriented-line degeneracies, shared endpoint cases, and winding of the chosen leaf handled explicitly. The upper construction showing realizability for beta arbitrarily close to sqrt(3) is not addressed here.

task_title: Final red-team check of the direct PL upper construction plus PL T-pattern lower bound
notes: The proof is sound. Recommended edits are expository: explicitly state local-finiteness closure in the lifted leaf-graph argument, note that leaf endpoints in the quotient boundary are distinct, and spell out that the target-triangle intersections in the 9-periodic construction coincide exactly with the quotient cell identifications.

The lower-bound half is complete and no unresolved gap remains in the PL degeneracy, leaf-graph, support-line, shared-endpoint, winding, or strict-inequality cases. The upper construction proving realizability for beta > sqrt(3) is not addressed here.
The PL upper bound beta > sqrt(3) is fully proved here. This does not address the separate lower bound beta >= sqrt(3), which is needed for the full infimum theorem.
